Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 82 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. NMAP in the Movies 22 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 179 upvotes, 25 comments
  4. Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation 17 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Can I run AI locally? 1333 upvotes, 323 comments
  6. Michael Faraday: Scientist and Nonconformist (1996) 31 upvotes, 3 comments
  7. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. Secure Secrets Management for Cursor Cloud Agents 32 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 566 upvotes, 167 comments
  10. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 393 upvotes, 122 comments
  11. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Python: The Optimization Ladder 135 upvotes, 36 comments
  13. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 145 upvotes, 42 comments
  14. Hammerspoon 324 upvotes, 118 comments
  15. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1005 upvotes, 407 comments
  16. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 46 upvotes, 13 comments
  17. Show HN: Ink – Deploy full-stack apps from AI agents via MCP or Skills 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  18. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 113 upvotes, 45 comments
  19. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 76 upvotes, 28 comments
  20. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 150 upvotes, 92 comments
  21. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 122 upvotes, 76 comments
  22. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 163 upvotes, 112 comments
  23. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 40 upvotes, 21 comments
  24. Everything you never wanted to know about visually-hidden 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  25. Nominal Types in WebAssembly 22 upvotes, 12 comments
  26. XML Is a Cheap DSL 179 upvotes, 163 comments
  27. Digg is gone again 320 upvotes, 325 comments
  28. Starlink Militarization and Its Impact on Global Strategic Stability 58 upvotes, 57 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 308 upvotes, 528 comments
